events.title: Bharatanatyam - Indian Classical Dance Performance
events.description: In honour of Asian Heritage Month, Bendale presents​ a performance of Bharatanatyam, the oldest dance tradition of India. Originating in the Southern part of India, the performance will highlight beautiful music, costume and story telling using hand gestures and expressions.  Drop-in All Ages. The program will take place in the Auditorium located in the basement. Elevator on site. If you have any questions, please call Bendale Branch at 416-396-8910.

events.title: Screen Time with Toronto Public Health
events.description: In this session, the benefits and impacts of screen time will be explored. events.title: Pawsitively Pets: Hands-on Animal Education
events.description: Pawsitively Pets is a hands-on animal education program for children of all ages. Our wonderful animal team is made up of an assortment of rescued pets that have found their forever home with us. All programs focus on responsible pet ownership, and the life-long commitment of owning a pet. Please note this program is suitable for children 5 years old up with adu

events.title: The Hidden Rivers
events.description: Participants will learn about the hidden rivers in Toronto and explore how our changing cityscape impacts on our waterways and ecosystems. Led by Shannyn Porter and Ursula Handleigh

Access and use of equipment will be on a first come, first serve basis.  Suitable for ages 6-12. Dash: May 3, June 7, July 5, August 9 LEGO: May 10, June 14, July 12, August 16 Snap Circuits: May 24, June 21, July 19, August 23 Sphero: May 31, June 28, July 26, August 30

events.title: Sunday STEM
events.description: Join us each week for a fun afternoon of exploring Science, Technology, Engineering and Math through small projects. Ages 6-12. Drop-in program, no registration required.

events.title: STEM Sundays
events.description: Explore a different STEM-themed activity every Sunday the library is open. Topics include robotics, circuitry, building and more! For children aged 6-12 and their caregivers. Drop in.

events.title: SUNDAY FUN-DAY
events.description: Looking for something FUN to do on Sundays! Come to our hands-on and collaborative learning program series; Lego Challenge, Robotics, Snap Circuits, and Board Games. For ages 6-12!

events.title: LEGO Club
events.description: Join us for an hour of LEGO fun! Use our guides to build a creation or use your creativity to build what you want!

events.title: Scavenger Hunt Sundays
events.description: Try your best to find all of the items hidden around the library each Sunday. Find them all and win a fun prize!

events.title: Snap Circuits
events.description: Explore electricity and circuitry during this hands-on program. Ages 6-12. Drop-in.

events.title: Sunday Lego Club
events.description: Join us each Sunday to build LEGO structures and have fun at the Forest Hill library! For children under 12 years and their families. Please ensure young children are accompanied by an adult.

events.title: Retro Movie: The Land Before Time
events.description: Five orphaned baby dinosaurs work together to find the Great Valley where they might live peacefully. Rated G. Ages 5-12. Drop-in.

events.title: Chess Club
events.description: All skill levels are welcome! Chess sets are provided.

events.title: Guildwood Chess Club
events.description: Join the Tuesday chess club at Guildwood Library , as we play chess every Tuesday starting on May 5 th . Players of all strengths and skill level are welcomed to participate in the club. Chess introduction lessons are available for new players that are new to the game. The program is for children ages 7+, teens, and adults . For More Information, please call the Guildwood Library at 416-396-8872. Drop-In. No registration required. events.title: Saturday Chess Club
events.description: Join us on the 2nd floor every Saturday for a few games of chess in a welcoming environment. All ages and all levels are welcome. Children must be accompanied by a parent or guardian to participate. Any unsupervised individuals under 18 will not be able to participate in the program. This is a drop-in program. Please note that there will be no chess club on Saturday May 16 for our Big Book Sale.

Ages 3-9. Drop-in.

events.description: Find your "inner magic" during this upbeat, hilarious, and interactive magical experience with BenTastic!  For children ages 4-12 and their caregivers Drop-in. No registration required.

events.title: Summer Wonder Camp: Presented by Dino Bites
events.description: Come join us at the library for a 90 min workshop for 3 days! Handle and discuss real and replica fossils of dinosaurs and other extinct beasts from all over the world. Discover the wild world of dinosaurs with an expert and learn about fossils evolution and the ‘Tree of Life.' Presented by Dino Bites. Ages 4-12.

events.title: Creative Writing Workshop
events.description: Join us for a fun and engaging workshop with award-winning author Melanie Florence. Learn techniques for brainstorming and transforming your creative ideas into stories. We'll explore plotting and characters so you can improve your writing skills and craft amazing stories of your own.  Ages 9-12. No registration required.

events.title: Anne of the Library-on-the-Hill
events.description: 'Growing up in the shadow of the Great War, Anne finds comfort in her neighbourhood library, where she loses herself in books, often imagining herself as part of the story. She particularly loves the books of LM Montgomery — and her imagination really takes off when she learns her beloved author is in town.' "Anne of the Library-On-The-Hill" (Plumleaf Press, August 2024), presented by the book's author Catherine Little, celebrates iconic Canadian author, Lucy Maud Montgomery, and her connection

events.description: Appleseed Yoga presents an interactive, movement-based program where stories come alive with our bodies! events.title: Botanical Photography / Sunprints
events.description: An alternative eco-friendly photography process that uses botanicals as both its subject matter and development process. I will teach participants how to make their own simple chemical-free photography developer and discuss alternative negatives. Participants will be encouraged to use bring plants from home, leaves found on site, etc to use for the process. This safe chemistry-free activity is adaptable for a broad group of ages Led by Ursula Handleigh

events.title: STEAM Club
events.description: Looking for something to do after school? Come out to STEAM Club and learn new things, make stuff, meet other kids, and have fun! April 13 - KEVA Challenges April 20 - Snap Circuits* April 27 - Fluttering Butterfly Craft* *12 spots. Equipment/Materials are limited on a first-come-first serve basis or until capacity is reached. May 4 - Bluebots 1 ** May 11 - Bluebots 2 ** May 25 - Rainbow Crafts **Please register for Bluebots 1 & 2 via the links above. 12 spots. Registration begins April 20.

events.title: The Hidden Rivers
events.description: Participants will learn about the hidden rivers in Toronto and explore how our changing cityscape impacts on our waterways and ecosystems. Looking at archival images and current references, participants will be guided to bring their own stories into our city’s images using material such as watercolours, crayons, playdough, text, etc onto colouring pages. Led by Shannyn Porter and Ursula Handleigh

events.title: Ewww...that Science is Gross!
events.description: Discover the science behind things that make you go...ewww! Make "snot" and sneeze it while exploring viscosity, identify various animals from their scat (i.e. poop) and learn about digestion while producing a burp in a bag. Join us on a gross but fun-filled science adventure!

events.title: Youth Hub: Ps5 2K26 Tournament
events.description: Drop into the Hub for a week of intense matches, strategic gameplay of Ps5 2k26 and Fc26 tournaments. Winners will win a prize! This program is part of Youth Hub service. Youth Hubs are a welcoming place for teens after school and in the summer, where helpful staff are always available. Free, nutritious snacks are offered daily. Drop by a branch with a Youth Hub or call the branch to find out more.

events.title: Loops, Swoops and Curlicues Exhibition Tour
events.description: How well can you read handwritten items? This free guided tour of Loops, Swoops and Curlicues: A legacy of handwritten history  will lead your through hundreds of years of handwritten items from TPL Special Collections.  Come test your decoding skills as we explore several hundred years of handwritten words. Registration is encouraged, but not required. There is space for drop-in guests. Please meet at the entrance to the TD Gallery.  Information Collection Notice: The personal in

events.title: Tween Hour- Minute to Win it!
events.description: Think you have what it takes to beat the clock? Join us for Minute to Win It , a fast-paced, laugh-out-loud challenge event where participants compete in a series of 60-second games.  Ages 11-14 years.  Space is limited. Drop in. First come first serve.

events.title: Youth Hub: What You Make It
events.description: This fun and exciting program will feature different types of crafting mediums to make your own craft! Use your creativity and design any craft you desire! This program is part of Youth Hub service. Youth Hubs are a welcoming place for teens after school and in the summer, where helpful staff are always available. Free, nutritious snacks are offered daily. Drop by a branch with a Youth Hub or call the branch to find out more.

events.title: Game On!
events.description: Level up your free time at the library after school! Teens are invited to drop in, hang out, and connect with friends in a relaxed, welcoming space. Dive into some casual play or friendly competition with popular video games for Switch and PS4/5, or enjoy some unplugged fun with board games when you need a screen break.

events.title: Youth Hub: Perler Beads
events.description: Make some fun designs of your favourite characters and turn them into keychains. This program is part of Youth Hub service. Youth Hubs are a welcoming place for teens after school and in the summer, where helpful staff are always available. Free, nutritious snacks are offered daily. Drop by a branch with a Youth Hub or call the branch to find out more.

events.title: Youth Hub: Board Game Café
events.description: Chill and learn to play various kinds of board games. There are single player board games to multi-player games to choose from. Youth Hubs are a welcoming place for teens after school and in the summer, where helpful staff are always available. Free, nutritious snacks are offered daily. Drop by a branch with a Youth Hub or call the branch to find out more.

events.description: Give books a second chance and repurpose pictures and more into a beautiful collage. This program is part of Youth Hub service. Youth Hubs are a welcoming place for teens after school and in the summer, where helpful staff are always available. Free, nutritious snacks are offered daily. Drop by a branch with a Youth Hub or call the branch to find out more.

Youth Hubs are a welcoming place for teens after school and in the summer, where helpful staff are always available. Free, nutritious snacks are offered daily. Drop by a branch with a Youth Hub or call the branch to find out more.

events.title: Youth Hub: Video Games
events.description: Drop in to the Youth Hub every Tuesday and Thursday to play video games. Play with friends, by yourself, or maybe someone new.  Tuesdays will be Nintendo Switch days and Thursdays will be PS4 days.  *NEW* Starting at 3:30 pm, teens can sign up for a time slot to use the consoles. If there is no demand, gaming will be awarded on a first come first serve basis.  This program is part of Youth Hub service. events.title: Youth Hub: Board Game Café
events.description: Join us in the Youth Hub every Tuesday to play board games with your friends! Ask us for our list of board games, and check our monthly calendar for Tournaments with prizes! All materials provided.  This program is part of Youth Hub service. Youth Hubs are a welcoming place for teens after school and in the summer, where helpful staff are always available. Free, nutritious snacks are offered daily. Drop by a branch with a Youth Hub or call the branch to find out more.

events.title: *Registration Full* Basic Tech Help: Seniors E-Connect
events.description: Are you 55+? Do you have a phone, tablet or computer you don't know how to use or want to learn to use better? Do you enjoy meeting new people? Seniors E-Connect will help you learn tech topics in a fun group setting, but with the one-on-one help of a teen volunteer. events.title: Youth Hub: Yarn Worry Dolls
events.description: Join us for Mindful Monday to make a worry doll with yarn. Originating from the Guatemalan Mayan tradition, worry dolls were handmade to help youth manage their fears and anxieties. While traditionally made from wire, wood, and colourful textiles, we will make our own version with soft yarn. Whisper your anxieties to your doll, place it under your pillow at night, and let your doll take your worries away by morning. This is a drop-in Youth Hub program for teens.  All materials provided

events.title: AI and the Environment with Han Qiao
events.description: Join our Innovator in Residence, Joel S. Silver, in conversation with Han Qiao about AI and the environment. Han’s current research sits in the intersection of human-computer interaction, design and urban planning. The program will take place in the Auditorium located in the basement. Elevator on site. If you have any questions, please call Bendale Branch at 416-396-8910.

This class includes hands-on instruction with Prusa Slicer, the software used to prepare your 3D object file for printing on a Prusa MK3S+ 3D printer. Note: Customers are no longer required to attend a mandatory 3D printing class prior to using a 3D printer at the Toronto Public Library’s Digital Innovation Hubs. This class is for those who are interested in learning more about 3D printing a

events.title: Art Journaling
events.description: Join us for an hour of creative art journaling fun! An art journal can include writing, drawing, painting, collage, stickers and more! No experience necessary, all materials will be provided. This is a drop-in program for all ages, no registration required.

events.title: Youth Hub: Air-Dry Clay Jewelry Dish
events.description: Learn how to make a cute, personalized jewelry or trinket dish using air-dry clay. Part 1 will consist of constructing the dish, and Part 2 will consist of 11decorating and glazing it. Participants must be available on both dates to complete the project.  This program is part of Youth Hub service. events.title: Sunday Family Game Time
events.description: Join us for a family games afternoon from Lego to Uno.  Every Sunday afternoon all ages welcome

events.title: Sunday STEAM Stations
events.description: Build, create, learn & play at various stations including Snap Circuits, LEGO, KEVA Planks, Connectors, & Colouring! This is a program for kids, teens, & families.  All are welcome!  Drop-in, no registration required. Please note: there is no program on Sun May 17, as TPL will be closed for observation of the Victoria Day holiday.

events.title: Youth Hub:  Nail Night
events.description: Unleash your creativity as you design your own stunning nail art! Join us for one or all of our fabulous Nail Nights on the first Monday of every month. Drop-in, no registration required. This program is part of Youth Hub service. Youth Hubs are a welcoming place for teens after school and in the summer, where helpful staff are always available. Free, nutritious snacks are offered daily. Drop by a branch with a Youth Hub or call the branch to find out more.

events.title: Teen Game Night
events.description: Drop-in every first Monday of the month to play our Xbox One S and board games! All games available on a first come, first serve basis. Hang out, and connect with friends in a relaxed, welcoming space. Teen nights happen every first Monday of the month.

events.title: Youth Hub: Drag Bingo with Messy Margaret
events.description: Kick off Pride Month with a bang! Join Toronto’s Delusional Diva, Messy Margaret, for some campy, lively Bingo and dazzling performances. All materials provided. This program is part of Youth Hub service. Youth Hubs are a welcoming place for teens after school and in the summer, where helpful staff are always available. Free, nutritious snacks are offered daily. Drop by a branch with a Youth Hub or call the branch to find out more.

events.title: Game On!
events.description: Level up your free time at the library after school! Teens are invited to drop in, hang out, and connect with friends in a relaxed, welcoming space. Dive into some casual play or friendly competition with popular video games for Switch and PS4/5, or enjoy some unplugged fun with board games when you need a screen break.

events.title: Youth Hub: Video Games

events.title: Discovery Club: Dungeons and Dragons
events.description: Learn the basics of the popular fantasy role-playing game – create your character and learn about game play including dice rolls, scoring, and imagination! Meet your Dungeon Master and participate in a weekly fantasy campaign as your created character. Will you complete your quest or get slayed by a vicious monster?  This program is for ages 10 to 14. events.title: Chess Club
events.description: All skill levels are welcome! Chess sets are provided.

events.title: POV: You Found a Good Book
events.description: Not your average book club. Think less assigned reading, more good vibes, real talk, and stories that actually hit. No pressure, no quizzes—just snacks, chill convos, and books you might actually want to finish. Come hang out, meet people your age, and help pick what we read next. You might even find a book you don’t hate.  Drop in. Bring a friend. Or just bring yourself—we saved you a spot. The Hate U Give

events.description: Drop in for a casual game of chess in a welcoming environment. Improve your chess skills or learn how to play. Sets are provided. All ages. Drop-in.

events.description: Join us for this two (2) session workshop where teens will explore watercolour painting techniques. All supplies provided.  Thursday, May 7th, 2026 &  Thursday, June 4th, 2026 DROP-IN!

events.title: Throwback Thursdays: Great Movies on the Big Screen
events.description: Come join us for a radical retro rewind projected on the big screen. Whether you're a first-time viewer or long-time fan, these movies are sure to please. April 16: Meet the Parents (2000, PG) April 23: Rounders (1998, R) April 30: No Country for Old Men (2007, 18A) May 7: Apollo 13 (1995, PG) May 14: The Martian (2015, PG) * special start time! 5:30 pm May 21: Contact (1997, PG) * special start time! 5:30 pm May 28: Star Trek (2009, PG) All are welcome. Drop-in, no registration required.

events.description: The Hillcrest Library and its neighbourhood is home to a variety of trees. Join the City of Toronto Urban Forestry staff to explore the natural world on a local neighbourhood tour. Learn how to recognize different species and fun facts about their cultural and ecological importance. Children under 12 must be accompanied by a parent/caregiver. Registration is required. events.title: Henna Art Workshop *Teens Only*
events.description: Discover the art of henna, decorative designs which are created on a person's body, using a paste of natural organic henna. Observe and enjoy the henna application and learn about its history, recipe and traditions. Led by Henna Artist, Hina Yasir.

events.title: Youth Hub: Video Game Café
events.description: Join us in the Youth Hub every Friday to play video games on our Nintendo Switch or PS5 with your friends! Ask us for our list of video games, and check our monthly calendar for Gaming Tournaments with prizes! This program is part of Youth Hub service. Youth Hubs are a welcoming place for teens after school and in the summer, where helpful staff are always available. Free, nutritious snacks are offered daily. Drop by a branch with a Youth Hub or call the branch to find out more.

events.title: Friday Teen Social
events.description: Drop in after school on Friday's to have fun with your friends, meet new folks, build connections, and tell us about programs and services you would like to see in the neighborhood!

events.title: Chess Club
events.description: Join us at the Bloor/Gladstone Library to play chess with friends, family, and fellow enthusiasts! Players of all ages and strengths are welcome, no previous experience is required. Drop in. No registration required.

events.title: Seniors E-Connect
events.description: Are you 55+? Do you have a phone, tablet or computer you don't know how to use or want to learn to use better? Do you enjoy meeting new people? Seniors E-Connect will help you learn tech topics in a fun group setting, but with the one-on-one help of a teen volunteer.

events.title: Pride Planet Cross Stitch
events.description: Join us at the Merril Collection for an introductory cross stitch workshop. Participants will stitch a Pride flag inspired ringed planet. Enjoy our current exhibit, Solar System Stories , and learn a new skill. This workshop is designed for beginners with no sewing or cross stitch experience. All supplies will be provided. This workshop will be offered on two different dates. Please only register for one. Information Collection Notice: The personal information on this form is collected by the To

events.title: Saturday Chess Club
events.description: Join us on the 2nd floor every Saturday for a few games of chess in a welcoming environment. All ages and all levels are welcome. Children must be accompanied by a parent or guardian to participate. Any unsupervised individuals under 18 will not be able to participate in the program. This is a drop-in program. Please note that there will be no chess club on Saturday May 16 for our Big Book Sale.

events.title: Steelpan: Hands On Fun!
events.description: Musician Suzette Vidale leads participants in a hands-on experience with the steelpan! ​Have fun while learning about the instrument, the proper technique of holding sticks, stance and playing a scale. events.title: Saturday Knit & Crochet Circle
events.description: Want to learn how to knit or crochet with a fun group of people? Looking for help with your project? You can also share your projects with others, teach or learn a technique, and enjoy great conversation. All levels of expertise are welcome. Bring your own yarn, needles, and crochet hooks. All are welcome. Drop-in, no registration necessary.

events.title: From the Golden Age to the Modern Era: A Musical Odyssey of East Asia
events.description: This concert introduces the history of the yangqin and its diverse regional and folk traditions, guiding the audience on a musical journey across China through sound, storytelling, and performance. This performance will include selections from different eras and styles, along with brief introductions to provide an overview of the musical journey. events.title: The Future of AI in Entertainment with James Rowan
events.description: Generative AI is rapidly reshaping how creative work is imagined, produced, and shared. From image and video generation to writing, design, and virtual production, these tools are expanding what's possible while also raising important questions about authorship, labour, and creative identity. In this talk, James Rowan explores the current landscape of AI tools in the creative industries, focusing on how artists, designers, and media practitioners are beginning to integrate them into their workfl

events.title: Rulers in Space: Measuring Distances in the Universe
events.description: Have you ever wondered how we are able to measure distances in the Solar System, in our Galaxy, and beyond? Grab your cosmic ruler and embark on an exploration of the methods used to infer distances in the Universe — the result of centuries of innovative thinking by countless generations of astronomers. events.title: Botanical Photography / Sunprints
events.description: An alternative eco-friendly photography process that uses botanicals as both its subject matter and development process. I will teach participants how to make their own simple chemical-free photography developer and discuss alternative negatives. Participants will be encouraged to use bring plants from home, leaves found on site, etc to use for the process. This safe chemistry-free activity is adaptable for a broad group of ages Led by Ursula Handleigh

events.title: The Hidden Rivers
events.description: Participants will learn about the hidden rivers in Toronto and explore how our changing cityscape impacts on our waterways and ecosystems. Looking at archival images and current references, participants will be guided to bring their own stories into our city’s images using material such as watercolours, crayons, playdough, text, etc onto colouring pages. Led by Shannyn Porter and Ursula Handleigh

events.title: Soundscape
events.description: Next we will be inspired by the library’s architecture and happenings as we are guided by musician Aniqa Qadir to create a soundscape. Working in groups, visitors will be prompted to absorb the sounds of the building and led through the process of creating an audio journey. This playful session welcomes participants to break the whisper norm of a library and interrupt the space with their observations. Led by Aniqa Qadir and Ursula Handleigh

events.title: Seniors E-Connect
events.description: Are you 55+? Do you have a phone, tablet or computer you don't know how to use or want to learn to use better? Do you enjoy meeting new people? Seniors E-Connect will help you learn tech topics in a fun group setting, but with the one-on-one help of a teen volunteer.

events.title: Youth Hub: Mindful Mondays
events.description: Every Monday, enjoy a series of stress-relieving mindfulness activities, crafts, and games to help ease anxiety and give you a moment of Zen. Enjoy mindful colouring sheets, rainbow scratch art, mindfulness card decks, gratitude journals, and more. All materials provided.  This program is part of Youth Hub service. Youth Hubs are a welcoming place for teens after school and in the summer, where helpful staff are always available. Free, nutritious snacks are offered daily. Drop by

events.title: Youth Hub: Board Game Café
events.description: Join us in the Youth Hub every Tuesday to play board games with your friends! Ask us for our list of board games, and check our monthly calendar for Tournaments with prizes! All materials provided.  This program is part of Youth Hub service. events.title: Knitting and Crochet Circle
events.description: Spend an afternoon at our weekly drop-in knitting and crochet circle! Bring your work in progress and enjoy visiting with other knitters and crocheters. A great place to show off your work, get advice, swap ideas, or just relax and visit with fellow fiber enthusiasts. Beginners and all ages welcome! A limited supply of materials will be available.

events.title: Youth Hub: Crafty Thursdays
events.description: Join us in the Youth Hub every Thursday for a series of crafting activities like painting, clay sculpting, sewing and embroidery, resin art, jewelry making, perler beads, block printing, and more! Youth Hubs are a welcoming place for teens after school and in the summer, where helpful staff are always available. Free, nutritious snacks are offered daily.
